us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: House passes $1.15 trillion defense policy bill
bullets: The House passed a defense policy bill authorizing about $1.15 trillion for national security.
The measure includes language to rename the Department of Defense as the Department of War.
Democrats objected to the bill’s cost and provisions tied to the Iran war and Israel cooperation.

articles.title: House Republicans approve $95 billion package for Iran war and Trump priorities
bullets: House Republicans adopted a $95 billion package tied to the Iran war and other administration priorities.
Speaker Mike Johnson moved the package through despite expected skepticism in the Senate.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told senators the Pentagon is running short of cash.

articles.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear pact depends on Israel normalization
bullets: President Donald Trump said a U.S.-Saudi civilian nuclear agreement requires Saudi Arabia to normalize relations with Israel.
Trump said the deal would not allow uranium enrichment.
Nonproliferation concerns have intensified around the emerging U.S.-Saudi agreement.

articles.title: Justice Department demands New Jersey noncitizen voter-registration data
bullets: The DOJ asked New Jersey for data on roughly 6,600 noncitizens the state says were accidentally registered to vote.
The department also requested information on about 400 people New Jersey said cast ballots.
The demand is part of the administration’s broader election-security push.

articles.title: U.S. judge sets June 2027 trial date for Nicolás Maduro
bullets: A Manhattan federal judge set a June 1, 2027 trial date for ousted Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro and Cilia Flores.
Both have pleaded not guilty to U.S. drug-trafficking charges.
Maduro’s lawyer said he would seek dismissal on immunity grounds.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Top worldwide developments from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Houthis attack Saudi tankers in Red Sea, threatening new oil chokepoint
bullets: Yemen’s Houthis said they attacked Saudi oil tankers Encelia and Layla in the Red Sea.
AP reported the attacks threaten Saudi shipments from Yanbu and add pressure alongside the Strait of Hormuz disruption.
Maritime analysts warned the attacks could widen risks to Europe-Asia trade routes.

articles.title: Rubio and Lavrov meet in Manila on Ukraine war diplomacy
bullets: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov at ASEAN meetings in Manila.
Rubio said the U.S. is ready to help end the war in Ukraine but warned there is no quick deal ahead.
The meeting took place alongside wider talks involving Asian and global foreign ministers.

articles.title: Wildfire near French tourist town forces 20,000 evacuations
bullets: A wildfire spread near a popular French tourist area, prompting authorities to move about 20,000 people to safety.
AP reported wildfires also continued across Sicily.
Italy’s national fire department said a firefighter was killed during operations in Caltanissetta.

articles.title: Russia says it struck Ukrainian ports and ships
bullets: Russia said it struck Odesa and Chornomorsk in Ukraine.
Moscow said it hit port infrastructure, loading facilities and vessels it alleged were supporting Ukraine’s military.
The reported strikes add to growing risks around Black Sea shipping.

articles.title: Heavy rain and flooding kill 82 across India, Pakistan and Afghanistan
bullets: Reuters reported at least 82 deaths from heavy rains and flooding across the three countries this week.
Assam reported severe flooding, with hundreds of thousands affected across multiple districts.
Authorities cited damage to farmland, infrastructure and public facilities.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Modi promises fast-track courts for exam paper leak cases
bullets: Prime Minister Narendra Modi said India would create fast-track courts to prosecute exam paper leak cases.
Protesters and opposition parties continued demanding Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation.
Reuters reported opposition disruptions in Parliament continued over the issue.

articles.title: India’s Cockroach protest movement holds capital sit-in
bullets: AP reported the youth-led protest movement continued a sit-in in New Delhi over alleged exam leaks.
Demonstrators demanded exam reforms, compensation for affected families and the education minister’s resignation.
Police had used tear gas and batons earlier as protesters tried to march toward Parliament.

articles.title: Assam floods deepen as regional death toll rises
bullets: Reuters reported at least 10 deaths in Assam over 24 hours, bringing the week’s state toll to 36.
More than 650,000 people were affected across 11 Assam districts, according to disaster officials cited by Reuters.
Flooding was linked to heavy rainfall and unusually high river flows from neighboring Nagaland.

articles.title: Rupee holds near lows as oil rally pressures India
bullets: The rupee was little changed Thursday as oil prices surged on Middle East tensions.
Reuters reported likely RBI intervention helped blunt pressure on the currency.
Higher energy prices threaten to lift inflation, slow growth and widen India’s current account deficit.

articles.title: Parliament deadlock continues over paper leak crisis
bullets: Both Houses of Parliament were adjourned amid opposition protests over exam paper leaks.
India Today reported government ministers defended Modi’s fast-track court announcement.
Opposition parties accused the government of avoiding accountability for the education crisis.

stocks.label: US Stocks
stocks.subtitle: Top US market movers from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Alphabet slips as AI spending worries overshadow cloud strength
bullets: Reuters reported Alphabet shares traded lower after earnings.
Alphabet posted its strongest-ever quarter of cloud growth.
Investors focused on higher AI spending plans and free-cash-flow pressure.

articles.title: Tesla falls after earnings show negative free cash flow
bullets: Tesla shares were lower after its second-quarter earnings release.
Reuters reported investors reacted negatively to Tesla’s AI infrastructure, battery, robotaxi and manufacturing spending.
Axios reported Tesla revenue of $28 billion, up 23% year over year.

articles.title: ServiceNow jumps after raising subscription revenue outlook
bullets: Reuters reported ServiceNow jumped 8.1% after raising its annual subscription revenue forecast again.
The company reported Q2 subscription revenue of $3.877 billion.
ServiceNow said subscription revenue grew 24.5% year over year.

articles.title: Rollins plunges premarket after Q2 miss and downgrades
bullets: Rollins shares fell sharply in premarket trading after reporting second-quarter results.
StreetInsider reported the miss triggered analyst downgrades.
The company released Q2 results on July 22 and held its call Thursday morning.

articles.title: Allegion surges after strong Q2 beat and raised outlook
bullets: Allegion stock rose about 8% in pre-open trading after its Q2 results.
Adjusted EPS came in at $2.40 versus a $2.21 analyst consensus cited by Investing.com.
The security-products company raised its full-year 2026 outlook.

business.label: Business
business.subtitle: Top corporate and business stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Alphabet reports cloud strength but investors focus on AI capex
bullets: Alphabet topped expectations on cloud growth tied to AI demand.
Reuters reported the stock fell as investors focused on higher capital spending.
The results were among the first major Big Tech earnings reports of the season.

articles.title: Tesla revenue rises, but margins and cash flow weigh on shares
bullets: Axios reported Tesla Q2 revenue of $28 billion, up 23% from a year earlier.
Reuters reported Tesla shares fell after the company showed negative free cash flow.
Investors scrutinized spending on AI infrastructure, robotaxis and next-generation manufacturing.

articles.title: IBM releases second-quarter results after AI spending concerns hit software sentiment
bullets: IBM released its second-quarter 2026 results on July 22.
The company had warned earlier that customer spending was shifting toward AI infrastructure.
Reuters reported the warning pressured IBM and other software stocks.

articles.title: Allegion raises 2026 outlook after double-digit revenue growth
bullets: Allegion reported Q2 revenue of $1.1515 billion, up 12.7% year over year.
Adjusted EPS rose 17.6% to $2.40.
The company raised full-year revenue and adjusted EPS guidance.

articles.title: New U.S. tariff hits billions in Brazilian exports
bullets: A new 25% U.S. tariff took effect on a range of Brazilian goods.
Reuters reported the duties affect farm machinery, wood products, ethanol and apparel.
Brazilian government and industry estimates put exposed exports at $7 billion to $11 billion.

tech.label: Tech
tech.subtitle: Top technology stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: AMD to sell Anthropic tens of billions in AI servers
bullets: AMD will sell Anthropic tens of billions of dollars of AI servers, Reuters reported.
AMD also plans to invest up to $5 billion in the Claude maker.
The deal challenges Nvidia’s dominance in AI compute.

articles.title: Bezos pushes AI-centered Prime Video redesign
bullets: Jeff Bezos pushed Prime Video leadership to make AI central to the streaming service, Reuters reported.
The project is known internally as Lighthouse.
Amazon wants to showcase AI capabilities to Prime Video’s more than 200 million users.

articles.title: FCC bars device sales using risky Chinese components
bullets: The FCC voted to block U.S. sales of devices containing key hardware from Chinese firms deemed security risks.
The rule targets components from companies such as Huawei and ZTE.
FCC Chair Brendan Carr said the agency was closing a component-parts loophole.

articles.title: PsiQuantum signs $125 million DARPA quantum-computing deal
bullets: PsiQuantum signed a $125 million agreement with DARPA.
The award supports DARPA’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative.
The program aims to assess whether an industrially useful quantum computer can be built by 2033.

articles.title: ServiceNow reports strong AI platform-driven Q2 growth
bullets: ServiceNow reported Q2 subscription revenue of $3.877 billion.
The company said subscription revenue grew 24.5% year over year.
ServiceNow highlighted enterprise adoption of its AI platform for governing and scaling AI.
